content: Nature has many different arms races between prey and predator
True like the one between cuckoo birds and normal birds
Developed a way to hide one egg in another nest better vs develop ways to identify fake eggs
Yeah have heard of them, just didn't remember the name of the species
The cuckoo bird lay eggs in another nest and don’t do parents support they get other bird to raise them
But the sad things is that often when the cuckoo bird hatch from an egg they instinctively try to push out the real eggs and/or chicks to kill them and get rid of competition
And they often grow larger and bigger by nature as well and the surrogate parent bird think that is it the strongest bird and priorities feeding the cuckoo bird over its own chick
Leading them to starve and die if they not already weaken enough to get push out of the nest before they’re ready and fall to their death
Even copilot thought of Cuckoo birds 😄
I know on one hand that’s it’s just instinct for the baby cuckoo chick to push and get rid of ANY heavy weight around it
Like how a tiger eat other animals or snake eat mice
The cuckoo is different to the rest of the average predator imo
Nature is wilder than some people think
We did have bird mafia so…

embeds.title: Channel-billed Cuckoo - BirdLife Australia
embeds.description: The Channel-billed Cuckoo is the largest parasitic cuckoo in the world. It has a massive pale, down-curved bill, grey plumage and long barred tail.

embeds.title: Great Spotted Cuckoo (Clamator glandarius)
embeds.description: The great spotted cuckoo (Clamator glandarius) is a member of the cuckoo order of birds, the Cuculiformes, which also includes the roadrunners, the anis and the coucals. The genus name clamator is Latin for 'shouter' from clamare, 'to shout'. The specific glandarius is derived from Latin glans, glan

I got a letter.
The name on the envelope said ‘Arisu.’
My friend's name…
It’s ridiculous, couldn’t possibly be true…
That’s what I keep telling myself…
A dead person can’t write a letter.
Arisu died of that damn disease three years ago.
So then why am I looking for him?
Our ‘special place’…
What could he mean?
This whole town was our special place.
Does he mean the park on the lake?
We spent the whole day there.
Just the two of us, staring at the water.
Could Arisu really be there?
Is he really alive… waiting for me?
